note: M12b rung 2 probe-scale results (PyPy 3.11). The composition-fair baccarat-value-8 proposition through the multi-deck particle-filter posterior; a perfect card counter nets exactly zero, so every realized unit is order structure surviving copy ambiguity. Small trial counts (probe scale) — the trend is the deliverable, not tight error bars. shelves=10, target_value=8, threshold=0.02.
reproduce: env PYTHONPATH=src uv run --python pypy@3.11 --no-project python data/e28_multideck.py <particles> <scale>

rows.comment: the fix: two passes == 200-shelf equiv; channel collapses

findings: Copy ambiguity does NOT obliterate the order channel: 2- and 3-deck shoes through one 10-shelf pass leak a large, gate-clean edge (decks=2: +21 u/shoe, 18.2 bits, z -0.18).

The copy penalty is real and measured: bits/shoe fell 18.2 -> 9.3 from 2 to 3 decks DESPITE the larger stack (more cards alone would raise bits), and predicted-vs-realized z slid to -3.07 at 3 decks -- the 60-particle filter loses fidelity (particle impoverishment) as copy ambiguity grows.
The two-pass fix collapses the channel at multi-deck too (0 bets, 0.035 bits) -- consistent with E26/E27's single-deck result.

CONFOUND (flagged): a fixed 10-shelf machine mixes a bigger stack worse (Diaconis: adequate mixing ~ n^1.5 shelves), so absolute edge conflates the copy penalty with mixing adequacy. The clean isolation is a same-n distinct-vs-duplicated comparison (queued, next chunk).

THROUGHPUT WALL: the PF is O(particles x slots x cards)/shoe; 2-pass is +20x (200-shelf slot axis). PyPy (4.3x, bit-identical, suite green) makes probe scale feasible but an 8-deck production run needs the O(slots) assumed-density filter or many more particles -- next rung.
